message:
fix(auth): use atomic tempfile+rename for auth.json persistence (bd-3d1n1)

save_data_sync was overwriting auth.json in place under a file lock,
which could corrupt credentials on crash. Replaced with an atomic
write-to-temp + persist() (rename) strategy using tempfile::NamedTempFile.

- save_data_sync now writes to a NamedTempFile in the same directory,
  sets 0o600 permissions, calls sync_all(), then atomically renames
  via temp.persist(path)
- New save_data_sync_with_hook for testing failure injection
- load now opens a separate lock file (auth.json.lock) for shared
  locking, then reads the data file directly with fs::read_to_string
- New auth_lock_path, open_auth_lock_file, sync_parent_dir helpers
- Removed old in-place truncate/pad/set_len logic
- Test: save_uses_lockfile_and_preserves_original_on_persist_failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
